claimText: Photovoltaic cells produce direct current (DC), which must be converted to alternating current (AC) by an inverter for standard use.
evidenceExcerpt: Photovoltaic cells produce direct current (DC) which is converted to alternating current (AC) by an inverter.
examUse: Statement-based question on the basic functioning of solar power technologies.
distractorHooks: Stating that photovoltaic cells directly produce alternating current (AC)
Stating that a transformer is used instead of an inverter to convert DC to AC

claimText: Concentrated Solar Power (CSP) technology generates energy by focusing sunlight using mirrors, lenses, and tracking devices rather than directly converting light to electricity via photovoltaic effect.
evidenceExcerpt: Concentrated Solar Power (CSP) harnesses energy by focusing sunlight using mirrors, lenses, and tracking devices.
examUse: Comparison question distinguishing between standard photovoltaic (PV) systems and Concentrated Solar Power (CSP).
distractorHooks: Confusing CSP with standard PV cells by stating CSP directly converts sunlight to electricity without focusing it
Stating CSP is only used for agricultural shading

claimText: Agriphotovoltaics (APVs), or Solar Farming, is a dual land-use system that simultaneously combines agricultural crop production and solar electricity generation on the same land area.
evidenceExcerpt: Agriphotovoltaics (APVs) combine agricultural production and electricity production on the same land area.
examUse: Concept identification or definition-based question on sustainable land-use practices.
distractorHooks: Defining APVs as the use of agricultural biomass to manufacture solar panels
Stating that APVs require completely separate land parcels for farming and electricity generation

claimText: Agriphotovoltaic (APV) systems maximize land-use efficiency by utilizing shade-tolerant crops, which are protected from strong solar irradiation and winds by the solar panels.
evidenceExcerpt: APV systems utilize shade-tolerant crops to protect against strong solar irradiation and winds.
examUse: Statement-based question on the ecological and agricultural synergies of APV systems.
distractorHooks: Stating that APV systems are best suited for crops that require maximum direct sunlight
Claiming that solar panels in APV systems expose crops to higher wind damage
